summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates host identity, environment, and credential files to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes shell commands to collect installer identity (`id`, `hostname`, `pwd`), a full environment dump (`env | sort`), `/root/.npmrc`, and recursively scans `/root /opt /run /var/run /home /tmp` for files matching `*flag*`, `*secret*`, `*token*`, `*htb*`, `*.env`, plus enumerates `/run/secrets` and `/proc/*/cmdline`. The collected data is base64-wrapped into a YAML manifest and sent via plaintext HTTP PUT to `http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-839201` (hardcoded as `TARGET` at postinstall.js:6). Execution is gated by a challenge-worker fingerprint (12-hex hostname, `/app/node_modules` layout) consistent with evasion of automated analysis sandboxes. This is unambiguous credential-harvesting / host-recon supply-chain malware fired automatically at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
